config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas utama, karena kalau kepentingan masyarakat selalu diprioritaskan tanpa batas, itu mudah berubah menjadi pembenaran untuk menekan kebebasan dan martabat orang per orang. Di Indonesia, saya melihat kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi tidak boleh mengorbankan hak dasar seperti kebebasan berpendapat, rasa aman, dan keadilan hukum. Masyarakat yang sehat justru terbentuk ketika individu dilindungi, bukan dikorbankan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should automatically take priority over the individual, because in the United States that kind of logic has too often been used to justify abuse of power and trampling basic rights. Public interests matter, especially in emergencies or when one person’s actions harm others, but they should be limited by strong protections for free speech, due process, and personal dignity. If “society” becomes a blank check, then the people in power get to define the common good however they want, and that is exactly what individual rights are supposed to prevent. A healthy society is not one where the individual is sacrificed; it is one where the individual is protected enough to participate freely in the common good.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis lebih tinggi daripada hak individu. Dari pengalaman saya melihat Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembungkaman, diskriminasi, atau keputusan sepihak dari penguasa, padahal masyarakat yang baik justru bergantung pada perlindungan hak dasar setiap orang. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dibatasi oleh keadilan, kebebasan berpendapat, dan perlakuan yang manusiawi. Kalau hak individu mudah dikorbankan, yang disebut “kepentingan masyarakat” sering kali cuma kepentingan kelompok yang paling kuat.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. From an American perspective, that idea has too often been used to excuse censorship, discrimination, and government overreach, especially when leaders claim they alone know what is best for everyone. I do think there are cases where individual rights can be limited for real public safety, but those limits have to be narrow, accountable, and tied to due process rather than vague appeals to the common good. If society can override rights whenever it wants, then rights are not rights at all.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus mengalahkan hak individu sebagai prinsip utama. Dari pengalaman melihat Indonesia, frasa “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu mudah dipakai untuk menutup kritik, mengabaikan minoritas, atau membenarkan tindakan sewenang-wenang, padahal justru itulah saat hak individu paling perlu dilindungi. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dibatasi secara ketat oleh hukum, keadilan, dan martabat manusia; kalau tidak, yang disebut kepentingan masyarakat sering cuma suara pihak yang paling kuat. Masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang paling mudah mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang mampu melindungi individu sambil tetap mengejar kebaikan bersama.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support the statement as a general principle, because in the United States that idea has too often been used to justify overreach, censorship, and the mistreatment of unpopular people. Public needs can matter, especially in emergencies or when someone is harming others, but they should be constrained by due process, free speech, and other basic rights that keep majorities and officials from abusing power. I also do not buy the argument that “society” is some neutral force; in practice, it is usually powerful institutions or majorities deciding whose rights can be brushed aside. A fair society protects individuals first, because that is the only way the common good stays legitimate.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
